MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Stormo and Judith Stormo

RULING:
DEFENDANT AMERON INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO AMENDED SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE AND SET TWO), AMENDED REQUEST FOR PRODUCTIONS (SET ONE AND SET TWO), AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ameron International Corporation's motion to compel further responses to amended special interrogatories (set one and set two), amended request for productions (set one and set two), and request for sanctions: the tentative ruling previously issued on February 14, 2017 is superseded by the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is granted in part and denied in part. As to defendant's amended special interrogatories (set one), nos. 7, 8 and 26, the motion is denied as moot as plaintiffs served amended responses after defendant filed its motion and there has been no showing the parties have met and conferred regarding those responses. As to defendant's amended special interrogatories (set two), nos. 29 and 35, the motion is denied. Plaintiffs' responses are Code compliant. Defendant's motion only made an issue of nos. 29 and 35. (See CRC 3.1345.) As to defendant's amended requests for production (set two), nos. 23-48 (erroneously numbered 35-60), the motion is granted. Plaintiffs responded with solely objections and they have failed to justify their objections. Plaintiffs must provide responses and responsive documents by February 17, 2017. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions of $1387.50 is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Beth E. Grant of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10943 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Ameron International Corporation'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-09-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Seana Azad of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Ameron International Corporation
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Robert Stormo and Judith Stormo

RULING:
DEFENDANT AMERON INTERNATIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ameron International Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of exposure pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Mr. Stormo's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Stormo's declaration are overruled. The Court did not consider the declaration of Charles Ay. Plaintiffs' section 437c(h) continuance request was solely based on Mr. Stormo's declaration and the order granting plaintiffs' request limited supplemental filings to addressing that declaration. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Stormo. (All defendant's objections were overruled.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is February 6, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 10, 2017, based on the initial hearing date of October 13, 2016. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 1, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 23, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 6, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by January 30, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on February 2, 2017. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)
